message:
style(fcp-conformance): rustfmt + trim duplicated permissive-profile DNS assertion in sandbox test

Follow-up polish landed by another agent after the ratelimit +
sandbox conformance tests merged in 60fd65ec. Two changes, both
scoped to the test files:

ratelimit_token_bucket_conformance.rs
- Pure rustfmt: wrapped two over-long lines
  (`initial token should be available` assertion and the
  `TokenBucket::from_config(...)` builder chain) to stay under the
  100-column boundary the workspace rustfmt config enforces.

sandbox_isolation_conformance.rs
- Dropped a duplicate `lookup_network + ip_name_lookup::Host::resolve_addresses("example.com")`
  sanity check from the permissive-profile half of
  `wasi_raw_socket_hostcalls_fail_closed_when_mediation_is_required`.
  That branch still asserts that SocketAddrUse::connect succeeds
  against a concrete loopback address via the `allowed_network`
  instance, so the "permissive profile exposes raw sockets" contract
  is still covered — the removed lines duplicated the same surface
  via DNS rather than AddrUse. The mediated half retains its
  `resolve_addresses` assertion + "resolver-failure" substring check
  verbatim, so the "mediated profile must still disable raw DNS"
  contract is unchanged.
- Rewrapped one `ip_name_lookup::Host::resolve_addresses(...)` call
  for rustfmt.

No behavior change outside the test suite; the removal is a pure
coverage-reduction scoped to a single over-specified assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
